source_jobs.title: Staff SW Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Key Responsibilities:
1. Backend Development and Optimization
• Design and implement high-performance backend components, services, and libraries in C/C++/Python.
• Develop modular and reusable software libraries with clear APIs and documentation.
• Optimize memory usage, CPU cycles, and application performance through advanced debugging and profiling techniques.
• Develop multi-threaded and asynchronous applications using POSIX threads, Boost, or similar libraries.
2. System Design and Architecture
• Architect and build scalable, distributed backend systems to meet complex business requirements.
• Work on low-latency systems, implementing efficient networking protocols such as TCP, UDP, and custom protocols.
• Contribute to the design of RESTful or binary APIs, ensuring secure and seamless communication between components.
3. Embedded and System-Level Programming
• Write and optimize system-level code for Linux environments, focusing on performance and reliability.
• Develop hardware-interfacing applications, including booloader, drivers and kernel.
• Handle memory management, inter-process communication (IPC), and shared resources in constrained environments.
4. Performance Tuning and Debugging
• Perform low-level profiling and tuning of applications using tools like perf, VTune, gprof, or Cachegrind.
• Optimize memory management using custom allocators, object pooling, and stack memory usage.
• Use advanced debugging techniques, such as core dumps, stack traces, and hardware breakpoints.
• Identify and eliminate bottlenecks in CPU-bound, memory-bound, or I/O-bound processes.
• Tune compiler settings and write efficient algorithms to achieve optimal performance.
• Debug complex issues in production systems using gdb, strace.
5. Networking and Protocol Development
• Develop efficient network protocols and manage socket programming for server applications.
• Implement and troubleshoot networking layers, including SSL/TLS, WebSocket, and HTTP/2.
• Develop custom communication protocols for specialized use cases, such as real-time streaming or IoT.
• Optimize networking stacks using socket programming, zero-copy mechanisms, and protocol offloading.
• Collaborate on the design of custom communication protocols for real-time or distributed systems.
• Familiar with Layer2 802.1Q, VLAN, bridging, spanning tree, LLDP, LACP protocols.
• Familiar with Layer3 Router, Firewall, ACL protocols.
• Familiar with 802.11 wireless protocols.
6. Testing and Quality Assurance
• Write robust unit tests using frameworks like Google Test (gtest) or Catch2.
• Perform system-level testing to ensure reliability in production environments.
• Establish continuous integration pipelines for automated testing and deployment of C/C++/Python codebases.
8. Security and Compliance
• Implement secure coding practices to prevent vulnerabilities such as buffer overflows, race conditions, and memory leaks.
• Implement secure transport protocols (SSL/TLS) and encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit.
• Design fault-tolerant systems with redundancy, checkpointing, and automatic failover mechanisms.
• Perform threat modeling and integrate security measures into the development lifecycle.
• Ensure backend systems are secure from common vulnerabilities (e.g., OWASP Top 10).
9. Integration with Hardware and External Systems
• Develop backend services that interface with hardware or IoT devices, handling real-time data streams.
• Manage communication with hardware peripherals via protocols like SPI, I2C, or CAN.
10. Continuous Improvement and Innovation
• Stay updated with advancements in C/C++/Python standards and emerging technologies.
• Contribute to open-source projects or internal frameworks.
• Propose innovative solutions to improve development workflows, system performance, or user experience.



Required Qualifications:
• Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field.
• Experience: 10+ years of profession

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineer,Software Development
source_jobs.description:
As a Software Engineer, you will play a critical role in designing, developing, and maintaining the firmware for Access points. Working closely with the Software Tech Lead, you will help build high-performance, secure and scalable networking solutions that enable enterprise-grade connectivity.

This role requires strong expertise in embedded Linux, networking protocols, and system design. You will focus on developing control-plane and data-plane software, ensuring efficient packet processing, system performance, and seamless hardware integration. A strong understanding of packet flow in Linux and OpenWRT systems is essential.

This is an opportunity to work at the intersection of networking, cloud, and embedded systems, making a direct impact on enterprise-grade WLAN infrastructure.

Responsibilities

 - Implement control-plan and data-plan features, ensuing efficient packet processing and network performance.

 - Work closely with the Software Tech Lead to define system architecture, design patterns and implementation strategies.

 - Optimize firmware to ensure low-latency, high-throughput, and reliable operation across various networking environments.

 - Collaborate with the internal hardware and software teams to integrate various services for embedded networking systems.

 - Develop and maintain secure, efficient, and well-documented APIs for internal and external use.

 - Enhancing logging, monitoring, and debugging capabilities to improve system observability and troubleshooting.

 - Participate in code reviews, technical discussions, and continuous improvement initiatives to maintain software quality.

 - Ensure compliance with networking security standards and best practices.

 - Contribute to automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, and system validation efforts to ensure firmware stability.

Key Qualifications

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience in Wi-Fi Access point development

 - Experience with Wi-Fi, 802.11, WLAN and BLE protocols and chipsets

 - Experience with Layer 2 and Layer 3 protocols at depth.

 - Strong programming skills in C, shell scripting (Go, Rust or Zig experience is a plus).

 - Experience working with OpenWRT and other open-source networking firmware is highly desirable.

 - Deep understanding of Linux networking subsystems, system programming, and kernel-level development.

 - Must have expertise in packet flow in Linux and OpenWRT systems, with a strong grasp of both control path and data path optimizations.

 - Experience with debugging, fixing and optimizing enterprise WIFI performance is a must.

 - Ability to troubleshoot complex networking and backend issues.

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to collaborate in cross-functional teams.

 - Self-motivated with a strong sense of ownership and responsibility.

Education

 - BE or ME in EE, E&C, Computer Science.

source_jobs.title: Senior Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Senior Systems Engineer (プリセールスSE) – Japan

Enterprise networking is evolving fast - Wi-Fi 7, cloud-managed infrastructure, and Zero Trust security are reshaping how Japanese enterprises build and manage their networks. NETGEAR is at the center of that shift, and we are looking for a Senior Systems Engineer in Japan who wants to be at the front of it.

In this role, you will be the primary technical voice for NETGEAR in Japan - partnering with account managers, channel partners, and enterprise customers to design solutions, lead proof-of-concepts, and build the kind of trusted relationships that drive long-term business. You will work alongside a collaborative global systems engineering team, contribute directly to product development through field feedback, and play a meaningful role in expanding NETGEAR's presence across the Japan enterprise and ProAV markets.

This is not a back-office role. If you enjoy being in front of customers, solving complex technical problems, and seeing the direct impact of your work - this is the right opportunity.


WORK LOCATION

• Based in Japan (Tokyo area preferred)

• Hybrid work environment - remote with regular on-site customer visits

• Domestic travel required (approximately 25–50% depending on territory and projects)


WHAT YOU WILL DO

• Lead pre-sales technical consultation, solution design, product demonstrations, and proof-of-concepts for enterprise customers across Japan

• Design and support deployment of enterprise-grade networking solutions including Wi-Fi 6/6E/7, switching, routing, security, and cloud-managed platforms

• Present technical solutions clearly to diverse audiences - enterprise IT teams, channel partners, ProAV integrators, and senior stakeholders

• Own the technical handoff from pre-sales to post-sales, working closely with customers and NETGEAR Support on troubleshooting and escalation management

• Conduct RF planning and wireless network design using tools such as Ekahau or similar

• Design secure network architectures aligned with Zero Trust principles, including segmentation, role-based access, and secure onboarding

• Enable and support channel partners - MSPs, VARs, and distributors - through technical training, joint customer engagements, and solution guidance

• Promote cloud-managed networking capabilities including zero-touch provisioning, real-time monitoring, firmware lifecycle management, and network analytics

• Gather field insights and customer feedback and communicate them directly to Product Management to influence NETGEAR's product roadmap

• Stay current on competitive technologies, enterprise networking trends, and the evolving ProAV landscape in Japan


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS（必須条件）

• 5+ years of experience in a Systems Engineering, Network Engineering, or customer-facing technical role (e.g., プリセールスSE, 技術営業)

• Strong hands-on knowledge of Wi-Fi technologies - Wi-Fi 6, 6E, and 7

• Solid Layer 2/3 networking fundamentals - switching, VLANs, routing, QoS

• Strong troubleshooting skills across wired and wireless enterprise environments

• Business-level Japanese - primary language for all customer and partner engagement

• Functional English - used for internal communication and collaboration with global teams. Japanese is the priority; willingness to use both in daily work is what matters


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS（歓迎条件）

• Experience with cloud-managed networking platforms such as NETGEAR Insight or comparable solutions

• Knowledge of Zero Trust security concepts - identity-based access, segmentation, secure onboarding

• Experience with RF design tools such as Ekahau or Hamina

• Industry certifications - CCNA, CCNP, CWNA, or equivalent

• Experience working with channel partners, MSPs, or mid-market enterprise customers

• Familiarity with AV over IP solutions and the ProAV integration space

source_jobs.description:
Join our dynamic Engineering team and play a pivotal role in unifying our organization’s business operations. As a Salesforce Integration Engineer, you will be the architect of connectivity, bridging our core Salesforce platforms (Sales, Service, and Marketing Clouds) with a diverse landscape of critical business applications to enable a seamless, automated enterprise.

This role requires deep technical expertise in Salesforce APIs, middleware solutions, and system architecture to drive end-to-end connectivity. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams—including Finance, Engineering, Product, and Customer Support—setting architectural standards that ensure secure, scalable, and reliable data flow across our technical ecosystem, from traditional ERPs to modern cloud infrastructure.

As a hybrid role based out of either our Chennai or Bangalore hub, you will work from the office at least three days a week, providing the flexibility to balance in-person collaboration with remote work. Your contributions will be essential in automating complex business processes, reducing manual overhead, and powering a truly connected 360-degree view of our customers.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Integration Strategy & Architecture: Scope, design, and drive end-to-end integration architectures between Salesforce clouds and external systems, establishing foundational standards for scalable data exchange.

 - Infrastructure & Support Integrations: Connect Salesforce with our Engineering infrastructure running on various AWS technologies and enhance customer experience by integrating support tools such as IVR systems and Chatbots.

 - Collaboration & Requirements: Work closely with business stakeholders and product managers to translate complex business requirements into technical integration solutions that solve key operational problems.

 - Performance & Quality: Monitor integration performance, optimize data throughput, ensure data integrity across systems, and drive testing standards to improve the reliability of data synchronization.

 - Documentation & Mentorship: Create detailed technical documentation (data maps, sequence diagrams) and establish integration best practices. Share designs with technical leaders and mentor junior team members on integration standards.

Required Qualifications:

 - Experience: 5+ years of software development experience, with a significant focus on enterprise-level Salesforce integrations in a complex environment.

source_jobs.title: Staff Engineer Embedded Firmware
source_jobs.description:
Role Overview

We are seeking an Embedded Linux Firmware Engineer to develop next-generation Intelligent PDUs (Power Distribution Units) used in Audio/Video racks, enterprise IT racks, and edge infrastructure environments.

This role focuses primarily on embedded Linux application firmware, middleware services, backend feature development, and embedded web UI integration. The engineer will build customer-facing PDU functionality such as outlet scheduling, monitoring, telemetry, configuration management, sensor integration, and network management services by leveraging BSP and Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL) APIs.

The ideal candidate has strong experience in embedded Linux systems, C/C++ development, networking protocols, and feature-level embedded application development.

Responsibilities

Embedded Linux Application & Middleware Development

 - Develop embedded C/C++ applications and middleware services on Linux-based PDU platforms

 - Implement customer-facing features such as:
   
   - Outlet scheduling
   
   - Outlet grouping and sequencing
   
   - Power event monitoring
   
   - Threshold configuration
   
   - Sensor monitoring
   
   - Alerting and notifications

 - Design and maintain backend service architecture for long-running embedded applications

 - Integrate and utilize BSP/HAL APIs for hardware operations such as:
   
   - Outlet on/off control
   
   - Sensor access
   
   - Power telemetry retrieval

Networking & Remote Management

 - Implement and maintain:
   
   - SNMP (v1/v2c/v3)
   
   - Modbus TCP
   
   - MQTT
   
   - HTTPS/REST APIs
   
   - WebSocket or embedded web communication interfaces

 - Develop network configuration and management features:
   
   - DHCP/static IP
   
   - DNS/NTP
   
   - Remote/Cloud access
   
   - Device discovery

 - Ensure reliability and stability of long-running network services

 - Implement secure communication using TLS/certificate-based authentication

Embedded Web UI & System Integration

 - Collaborate with frontend/UI teams to integrate embedded web interfaces with backend services

 - Develop APIs and middleware supporting web-based monitoring and configuration

 - Implement persistent configuration storage for:
   
   - Outlet names
   
   - Schedules
   
   - Thresholds
   
   - Network settings

 - Support OTA/FOTA firmware update workflows and rollback mechanisms

Diagnostics, Logging & Reliability

 - Implement:
   
   - Event logging
   
   - Diagnostics
   
   - Power history
   
   - Fault/alarm reporting

 - Debug runtime issues involving:
   
   - Networking
   
   - Memory leaks
   
   - IPC
   
   - Long-running service stability

Sensor & Peripheral Integration

 - Integrate environmental and external sensors:
   
   - Temperature
   
   - Humidity

 - Work with communication interfaces such as:
   
   - UART
   
   - I2C
   
   - SPI
   
   - RS485
   
   - Ethernet

Collaboration

 - Work closely with BSP team, hardware team, QA, and cloud teams

 - Support system integration, validation, and production issue debugging

 - Participate in architecture discussions, code reviews, and feature planning

Required Skills

 - 10+ years of relevant experience

 - Strong proficiency in C/C++ for embedded systems

 - Strong Linux/Embedded Linux development experience

 - Experience with embedded application or middleware development

 - Experience with networking protocols:
   
   - TCP/IP
   
   - MQTT
   
   - HTTP/HTTPS
   
   - SNMP
   
   - Modbus

 - Experience with multithreading, IPC, synchronization, and long-running services

 - Experience debugging embedded systems using logs, gdb, logic analyzers, or oscilloscopes

 - Experience with UART, SPI, I2C, Ethernet, or RS485 interfaces

 - Familiarity with OTA/FOTA update mechanisms and persistent storage management

 - Experience with Git and Linux development environments

Preferred Skills

 - Experience with embedded web servers or embedded web UI integration

 - Experience with Yocto or Build

source_jobs.description:
About the Role:

As an Engineer II -System validation Test Automation, you will work closely with senior engineers to validate NETGEAR’s Insight along with Wireless Access points, Pro Routers, Switches and Security Devices. You will be responsible for testing both functional and performance aspects of networking and wireless features. You will contribute to our automation efforts using Python and Robot Framework and help integrate these tests into continuous delivery pipelines.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Design and execute test cases for either of Switches, Pro Routers, Access Points, and Security Devices running advanced wireless and network features.

 - Validate wireless networking features including 802.11 a/b/g/n/ac/ax/be, roaming, band steering, DFS, and mesh technologies.

 - Test and debug issues related to wireless security protocols (WPA2, WPA3), firewall behavior, VPN, and access control.

 - Validate wired/wireless interoperability across Layer 2/3 switches, routers, and end-user devices.

 - Develop and maintain automation test scripts using Python and Robot Framework.

 - Work with senior QA and development engineers to debug and reproduce issues.

 - Track defects using Jira, maintain test documentation in Confluence, and manage scripts in Git/GitHub.

 - Execute tests in simulated/Real environments with real Switches, access points, clients, and security gateways.

 - Participate in Agile activities and collaborate across cross-functional teams (embedded, cloud, cross functional QA).

Qualifications:

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Electronics, Telecommunications, or a related field.

 - 2-4 years of experience in QA/testing or networking

 - Strong interest or certification in Networking/Wi-Fi (e.g., CCNA, CWNA) is a plus.

 - Strong desire to grow in the field of networking technologies and QA automation.

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineer Embedded Firmware
source_jobs.description:
Role Overview

We are seeking an Embedded Linux Firmware Engineer to develop next-generation Intelligent PDUs (Power Distribution Units) used in Audio/Video racks, enterprise IT racks, and edge infrastructure environments.

This role focuses primarily on embedded Linux application firmware, middleware services, backend feature development, and embedded web UI integration. The engineer will build customer-facing PDU functionality such as outlet scheduling, monitoring, telemetry, configuration management, sensor integration, and network management services by leveraging BSP and Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL) APIs.

The ideal candidate has strong experience in embedded Linux systems, C/C++ development, networking protocols, and feature-level embedded application development.

Responsibilities

Embedded Linux Application & Middleware Development

 - Develop embedded C/C++ applications and middleware services on Linux-based PDU platforms

 - Implement customer-facing features such as:
   
   - Outlet scheduling
   
   - Outlet grouping and sequencing
   
   - Power event monitoring
   
   - Threshold configuration
   
   - Sensor monitoring
   
   - Alerting and notifications

 - Design and maintain backend service architecture for long-running embedded applications

 - Integrate and utilize BSP/HAL APIs for hardware operations such as:
   
   - Outlet on/off control
   
   - Sensor access
   
   - Power telemetry retrieval

Networking & Remote Management

 - Implement and maintain:
   
   - SNMP (v1/v2c/v3)
   
   - Modbus TCP
   
   - MQTT
   
   - HTTPS/REST APIs
   
   - WebSocket or embedded web communication interfaces

 - Develop network configuration and management features:
   
   - DHCP/static IP
   
   - DNS/NTP
   
   - Remote/Cloud access
   
   - Device discovery

 - Ensure reliability and stability of long-running network services

 - Implement secure communication using TLS/certificate-based authentication

Embedded Web UI & System Integration

 - Collaborate with frontend/UI teams to integrate embedded web interfaces with backend services

 - Develop APIs and middleware supporting web-based monitoring and configuration

 - Implement persistent configuration storage for:
   
   - Outlet names
   
   - Schedules
   
   - Thresholds
   
   - Network settings

 - Support OTA/FOTA firmware update workflows and rollback mechanisms

Diagnostics, Logging & Reliability

 - Implement:
   
   - Event logging
   
   - Diagnostics
   
   - Power history
   
   - Fault/alarm reporting

 - Debug runtime issues involving:
   
   - Networking
   
   - Memory leaks
   
   - IPC
   
   - Long-running service stability

Sensor & Peripheral Integration

 - Integrate environmental and external sensors:
   
   - Temperature
   
   - Humidity

 - Work with communication interfaces such as:
   
   - UART
   
   - I2C
   
   - SPI
   
   - RS485
   
   - Ethernet

Collaboration

 - Work closely with BSP team, hardware team, QA, and cloud teams

 - Support system integration, validation, and production issue debugging

 - Participate in architecture discussions, code reviews, and feature planning

Required Skills

 - 8-10 years of relevant experience

 - Strong proficiency in C/C++ for embedded systems

 - Strong Linux/Embedded Linux development experience

 - Experience with embedded application or middleware development

 - Experience with networking protocols:
   
   - TCP/IP
   
   - MQTT
   
   - HTTP/HTTPS
   
   - SNMP
   
   - Modbus

 - Experience with multithreading, IPC, synchronization, and long-running services

 - Experience debugging embedded systems using logs, gdb, logic analyzers, or oscilloscopes

 - Experience with UART, SPI, I2C, Ethernet, or RS485 interfaces

 - Familiarity with OTA/FOTA update mechanisms and persistent storage management

 - Experience with Git and Linux development environments

Preferred Skills

 - Experience with embedded web servers or embedded web UI integration

 - Experience with Yocto or Buil

source_jobs.description:
Role Overview:
We are seeking a skilled and motivated QA Network Test Engineer to validate complex networking features and ensure the high quality and reliability of our networking products. The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in L2 and L3 networking protocols and experience in both manual and automated testing in a networking domain.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Test Planning & Execution: Design, develop, and execute comprehensive test plans and test cases for L2/L3 networking protocols and features.

   - L2: VLANs
   
   - L3: IPv4/IPv6 forwarding, routing protocols.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Electronics, or a related technical field, or equivalent experience.

 - 6 to 8 years of hands-on experience in manual and automated testing of networking products.

 - Deep understanding and hands-on experience working with L2 and L3 networking protocols.

 - Proficiency in Python scripting for test automation.

 - Experience with networking test tools such as Ixia, Spirent, or Wireshark.

 - Familiarity with Linux/Unix environments and command-line operations.

 - Strong analytical, problem-solving, and debugging skills across data, control, and management planes.

source_jobs.title: Sr Systems Engineer  - Broadcast
source_jobs.description:
This role ensures NETGEAR becomes a credible and trusted networking partner in broadcast audio and small- to mid-scale video environments. The Broadcast SE provides the technical leadership required to validate solutions, design deployments, guide partners, and feed market requirements back into Product Line Management and Engineering. At the same time, this person maintains strong business awareness, proactively identifies growth opportunities, and supports Sales and Business Development in expanding NETGEAR’s broadcast presence.



Key Responsibilities

 - The Broadcast SE is responsible for developing and maintaining strong technical integrations with broadcast OEM partners such as Riedel, EVS, Clear-Com, Panasonic, Grass Valley, and others. This involves continuous testing, interoperability validation, participation in joint solution development, and representing NETGEAR in partner technical discussions.

 - The role includes designing, validating, and supporting real-world broadcast workflows, including AES67, Dante, Ravenna, NDI, ST2110, IPMX, and PTP-sensitive environments. The SE manages lab infrastructure, testbeds, and demonstration systems to ensure all solutions meet the expectations of broadcast engineers and production teams.

 - A critical responsibility is influencing and supporting commercial growth. The Broadcast SE works with Sales, Business Development, and Alliances to identify new opportunities, support major accounts, and proactively engage system integrators. The SE assists in developing proposals, participating in customer meetings, and ensuring NETGEAR is included in early design stages.

 - Another essential aspect is enablement. The Broadcast SE develops technical training for regional SE teams, creates internal and external documentation, and contributes to certification and partner enablement programs. This ensures field consistency and positions NETGEAR as a knowledgeable and reliable resource in the broadcast community.

 - The SE collaborates continuously with PLM and Engineering to escalate technical requirements, identify feature gaps, and provide detailed field feedback on switching features, firmware, multicast behavior, QoS, redundancy models, PTP accuracy, and integration needs with broadcast control systems. This collaboration supports roadmap alignment with real market needs as defined in the broadcast strategy.

 - Marketing support is also part of the role. The Broadcast SE participates in industry events such as NAB, IBC, and InfoComm; supports the creation of white papers, solution guides, and case studies; demonstrates solutions to press and analysts; and helps position NETGEAR as a knowledgeable contributor during the industry’s migration to IP.
   
   

Required Experience and Competencies

 - The ideal candidate brings a strong technical foundation in networking with real-world broadcast experience. They must be fluent in broadcast audio and IP video protocols, including AES67, Dante, Ravenna, SMPTE ST2110, Multicast, IGMP, QoS, and PTP. Experience designing or supporting production facilities, intercom systems, OB trucks, post-production networks, or enterprise studios is expected.

 - They should have meaningful exposure to business development, partner engagement, or technical sales. This role requires someone comfortable working independently in the field, developing relationships, uncovering new opportunities, and driving technical conversations that lead to commercial outcomes.

 - Strong communication, presentation, and customer-facing abilities are essential. The Broadcast SE will regularly interface with engineers, executives, and partners and must be able to simplify complex architectural concepts while establishing technical credibility.

 - Experience collaborating with product and engineering teams is important. The candidate must be able to articulate market needs clearly and translate customer requirements into actionable product feedback.

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineer 1
source_jobs.description:
As a Software Engineer, you will play a critical role in designing, developing, and maintaining the firmware for Access points. Working closely with the Software Tech Lead, you will help build high-performance, secure and scalable networking solutions that enable enterprise-grade connectivity.

This role requires strong expertise in embedded Linux, networking protocols, and system design. You will focus on developing control-plane and data-plane software, ensuring efficient packet processing, system performance, and seamless hardware integration. A strong understanding of packet flow in Linux and OpenWRT systems is essential.

This is an opportunity to work at the intersection of networking, cloud, and embedded systems, making a direct impact on enterprise-grade WLAN infrastructure.

Responsibilities

 - Implement control-plan and data-plan features, ensuing efficient packet processing and network performance.

 - Work closely with the Software Tech Lead to define system architecture, design patterns and implementation strategies.

 - Optimize firmware to ensure low-latency, high-throughput, and reliable operation across various networking environments.

 - Collaborate with the internal hardware and software teams to integrate various services for embedded networking systems.

 - Develop and maintain secure, efficient, and well-documented APIs for internal and external use.

 - Enhancing logging, monitoring, and debugging capabilities to improve system observability and troubleshooting.

 - Participate in code reviews, technical discussions, and continuous improvement initiatives to maintain software quality.

 - Ensure compliance with networking security standards and best practices.

 - Contribute to automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, and system validation efforts to ensure firmware stability.

Key Qualifications

 - 8+ years of software engineering experience in Wi-Fi Access point development

 - Experience with Wi-Fi, 802.11, WLAN and BLE protocols and chipsets

 - Experience with Layer 2 and Layer 3 protocols at depth.

 - Strong programming skills in C, shell scripting (Go, Rust or Zig experience is a plus).

 - Experience working with OpenWRT and other open-source networking firmware is highly desirable.

 - Deep understanding of Linux networking subsystems, system programming, and kernel-level development.

 - Must have expertise in packet flow in Linux and OpenWRT systems, with a strong grasp of both control path and data path optimizations.

 - Experience with debugging, fixing and optimizing enterprise WIFI performance is a must.

 - Ability to troubleshoot complex networking and backend issues.

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to collaborate in cross-functional teams.

 - Self-motivated with a strong sense of ownership and responsibility.

Education

 - BE or ME in EE, E&C, Computer Science.

source_jobs.title: Sr. Systems Engineer, ProAV - South Central
source_jobs.description:
The Sr. Systems Engineer, ProAV serves as a technical expert supporting NETGEAR's ProAV solutions across the South Central U.S. This role combines customer onboarding, field engineering, pre-sales support, and industry engagement. The ideal candidate brings deep expertise in AV-over-IP networking, strong troubleshooting capabilities, and the ability to deliver high-impact technical training and solution design.

This is a remote-based position requiring up to 60% travel throughout the region.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Customer Onboarding & Technical Training

 - Lead onboarding for U.S. customers implementing NETGEAR ProAV switches and third-party integrations

 - Collaborate with the South Central Sales Territory Manager to ensure successful deployments

 - Deliver remote and on-site training covering AV-over-IP network design, switch profile selection, PTP configuration, Dante/AVB/Milan configuration, and API integration and video configuration

 - Develop customized training materials, system designs, and internal/external knowledge base documentation

 - Ensure successful transition into production environments, including complex multi-switch and multi-vendor deployments

Professional Services & Field Engineering

 - Provide advanced on-site troubleshooting for high-profile customers experiencing complex timing, multicast, or interoperability issues

 - Capture logs, perform packet traces, replicate issues in lab environments, and provide real-time diagnostics

 - Support commissioning of AV-over-IP systems to ensure stable production performance

 - Resolve escalated issues involving AVB, Dante/AES67, NVX, Q-SYS, SMPTE-2059-2, PTPv2, and multicast networking

Pre-Sales Engineering & Business Expansion

 - Support Sales, Business Development, and Channel teams with technical scoping and solution design

 - Conduct on-site and virtual product demonstrations

 - Assist in Bill of Materials (BOM) creation and system architecture design

 - Provide competitive analysis and technical positioning

 - Contribute technical expertise to expand NETGEAR's ProAV presence in the U.S. market

Internal Tools & Documentation Development

 - Develop and maintain internal scripts, configuration tools, and deployment utilities

 - Create automation tools to reduce troubleshooting time, including log analysis, profile validation, and configuration parsing

 - Author best practice guides, training materials, and knowledge base documentation

Industry Representation & Events

 - Represent NETGEAR at U.S. industry conferences and partner events

 - Deliver technical workshops and presentations

 - Provide advanced AV-over-IP education and roadmap overviews

 - Support event-based network deployments and multi-vendor interoperability demonstrations

QUALIFICATIONS

 - Minimum 5 years of experience in networking systems, AV-over-IP technologies, or related systems engineering roles

 - Deep knowledge of ProAV networking products, AV-over-IP technologies, multicast networking and IGMP, and switch profile development

 - Strong understanding of routing and switching fundamentals

 - Hands-on deployment experience with AVB, Dante, Q-SYS, Crestron, SMPTE-2059-2, and multicast networks

 - Field experience troubleshooting complex AV-over-IP systems

 - Independent lab development and advanced technical self-study

Preferred Qualifications

 - Bachelor's degree preferred but not required

 - Industry certifications such as CCNA, CWNA, AVIXA CTS, Dante, or AVB are beneficial but not required

 - Experience working in a Solutions Architect or Systems Engineering capacity preferred

Travel Requirements

• Must be willing to travel up to 60% within the South Central U.S. region.



Company Statement/Values:  

At NETGEAR, we are on a mission to unleash the full potential of connectivity with intelligent solutions that delight and protect. We turn ideas into innovative networking products that connect people, power businesses, and advance the way we live.

notes: NETGEAR is a large networking company with a strong focus on engineering and product development. The job postings suggest a high volume of hiring in technical roles, with a mix of software, hardware, and networking engineering positions.
